description_long: This house features a medieval Tudor-style design with exposed wooden beams, stucco walls, and a steeply pitched, shingled roof. The structure is asymmetrical, with a prominent front gable and a secondary wing extending to the side. Multiple small, diamond-paned windows with dark frames are distributed across all visible facades, adding to the historic character. The exterior shows areas of exposed brickwork, particularly around the chimney and window sills, enhancing the rustic appearance. A hanging sign bracket is visible on one side, suggesting a possible mixed-use or historical function. The overall look is that of a traditional European townhome or inn, suitable for a dense urban or village setting.
description: A medieval Tudor-style house with exposed wooden beams, steep shingled roof, diamond-paned windows, and rustic brick accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main gable, multiple windows, and the entryway beneath the overhanging upper story.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long shingled roof, symmetrical window rows, and a hanging sign bracket.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear view of the chimney, symmetrical window arrangement, and exposed brickwork.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the intersecting rooflines, brick chimney, and additional windows.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with small diamond-paned windows
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed entry beneath overhanging upper story, no visible porch, medieval wooden door likely present
